The effect typically enhances/magnifies the character's special traits or helps mitigate some of their downsides. {| class="wikitable striped sortable mw-collapsible" data-description="Birthright Effects" style="min-width:350px; margin-right:10px;"

On pickup, fully heals Isaac and gives him a temporary +21.6 damage up. The damage upgrade scales down to 0 over the course of three minutes. Due to how the damage formula works, the damage upgrade scales down faster over time, starting at -~0.015 per half second and increasing to -~.1 per half second as the bonus approaches 0.
warunek: Defeat Satan as Jacob and Esau

opis: On use, everything that Isaac has, including stats, consumables, trinkets, and all items except for starting items and quest items, are removed. Isaac is teleported to a unique Clean Bedroom that acts like an I AM ERROR room. It has a Bed, two random Chests, two Grab Bags, and a random Trinket. The only way out is through the beam of light, which teleports Isaac to the next floor (with some exceptions, seen below).
warunek: Defeat Ultra Greedier as Jacob and Esau

opis: Gives five keys. On use, Isaac holds a key above his head. Pressing a fire button removes one key and throws it as a piercing projectile. The key damages enemies, as well as opens locked doors and locked chests it hits. The key will also break the first rock it hits, as well as Key Blocks. The thrown key does damage equal to 5× Isaac's damage + 30.
__TOC__ Booster Packs were recurring content updates for The Binding of Isaac: Afterbirth †. Booster Packs contained popular community-developed mods as updates in the game continued to add features.

Gigantifies Isaac for 30 seconds, persisting between rooms and floors. While Gigantic, Isaac gains the following effects; Gains a 4x damage multiplier, +2 range, and loses -1.9 tears. Cannot take damage for the duration of the effect. Can destroy objectsObjects, Obstacles, and Pitfalls without falling into them and open doors by walking into them. This includes metal blocks and key blocks without using keys.
warunek: Earn all Hard mode Completion Marks for all non-Tainted characters

opis: Spawns a knife handle familiar that follows Isaac. Generates a room in Mines / Ashpit II for Knife Piece 2 to spawn in. Combines with Knife Piece 2 to form a knife familiar that launches itself in the direction Isaac shoots, dealing 25 damage to and piercing enemies it hits. Launching it at the red door at the end of Mausoleum/Gehenna II will open the door, destroying the knife in the process.

Combines with Knife Piece 1 to form a knife familiar that launches itself in the direction Isaac shoots, dealing 25 damage to and piercing enemies it hits. Launching it at the red door at the end of Mausoleum/Gehenna II will open the door, destroying the knife in the process.

Takes Isaac to an abandoned and expanded version of Home, which has one copy of every item the player has unlocked on that save file. After taking an item, Isaac returns to the room he used Death Certificate in. There are also several pickups and chests in the area. Picking them up/opening them will not cause Isaac to leave the area.
warunek: Earn all Hard mode Completion Marks for all characters, including tainted ones

opis: Spawns a robotic Pooter familiar that orbits around Isaac and fires shielded tears at incoming enemy projectiles, similar to those from Lost Contact. The shielded tears home in on projectiles, but not enemies. The familiar deals 1 contact damage per tick or 3 damage per second, and instantly kills weak fly enemies on contact.

Upon use, splits enemies in the room into two smaller versions with ~40% health each. Normal enemies can be split twice, while champions and bosses can be split three times. If an enemy cannot be split, they instead take 25 damage. If the enemy splits as part of their mechanic, such as Squirt, Teratoma, or The Fallen, they will instead take the damage required to split them.

Gives Isaac +2.0 luck. Allows Isaac to stand in enemy creep for 1 second before taking damage. Makes Isaac immune to burn, confusion, fear, and toxic gas clouds. Additionally grants immunity to cobwebs, fireplaces (or flame attacks), and poison clouds from destroying mushrooms. This also includes the Godhead aura from Dogma's projectiles.
Dogma is a boss added in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance and is the first boss of Home.

Spawns a red crack in the ground somewhere in the room while Isaac is in a room with enemies. Walking over the crack summons a ghost that homes in on the nearest enemy, exploding when it hits it. After the ghost is summoned, cracks will keep showing up in the room until it is cleared. The ghost spends one second spawning in before it attacks.

While held, spawns a familiar that moves in the direction Isaac shoots. Activating the item causes Isaac to swap places with Stitches. Isaac is briefly immune to damage after teleporting. Stitches deals 1.5 contact damage to enemies per tick (3.0 damage per second). If Isaac teleports onto an enemy, they will take 40 + 3× Isaac's damage.

Returns Isaac to the beginning of the run in Chapter 1, with all stat boosts, items, pickups, and trinkets intact, similar to a Victory Lap. Unlike a Victory Lap, however, no effects are applied after a certain number of uses, and the timer is not reset.

Adds a chance to shoot red fist-shaped tears that deal extreme knockback and cause confusion for 2 seconds. Enemies will take 10 + 2 * (floor - 1) damage when they are knocked into a wall/obstacle or when hit by another knocked back enemy.

On use, Isaac holds an Eraser above his head, which can be thrown in any cardinal direction. Upon hitting a monster, the monster will be "erased", preventing any of the same monster from appearing for the rest of the run. Deals 15 damage to bosses. If fatal, erases the boss for the rest of the run. Can only be recharged by entering a new floor. Batteries and most other methods of recharging have no effect.

Grants a Rotten Heart upon use. If Isaac has an empty red heart container (or empty bone heart), the rotten heart will fill it. If Isaac has red hearts and/or bone hearts but no empty container, it will replace one of its red hearts. If Isaac doesn't have red or bone hearts, it has no effect.

On use, Isaac pulls out (or puts away) the urn; holding a fire button when it's pulled out emits a stream of blue flames in whichever direction Isaac is aiming. Each flame deals 22.5-31.5 damage per second, depending on how far it's traveled, plus an additional 4.5 damage per second times floor number. Multiple flames can damage the same enemy simultaneously.

While in a room with enemies, creates a trail of tears that Isaac drags behind him. Each tear deals 3.5 damage, regardless of Isaac's damage. The shorter the trail of tears is, the faster it grows. The trail is composed of 20 tears at max length. Once the room is cleared, the trail will remain but will stop regenerating.

Spawns an item from the current room's item pool. Consumes one of Isaac's heart containers, one bone heart, or two soul hearts, replaced by a broken heart container which can never be filled, permanently reducing his maximum possible health. The priority order of hearts that will be consumed is: one heart container, one bone heart, and two soul hearts.
warunek: Defeat Mother as Jacob and Esau

opis: Gives Isaac flight. Continuously firing for 2.35 seconds allows Isaac to fire a powerful holy laser when the fire button is released, similar to the attacks used by the Angel bosses. The laser deals Isaac's damage per tick (up to a maximum of 15 times) and can be aimed diagonally. It lasts for ~1.28 seconds (77 frames).

Gives Isaac one of the following stat boosts, depending on what attribute Isaac has the least of: +1.0 flat damage +0.5 fire rate +0.2 speed +2.5 range Also drops either 3 Penny, 1 bomb, or 1 key on the ground. Bombs and keys are valued at 3 coins' worth. The pickup dropped will be what Isaac has the least value of, and in case of a tie one is chosen randomly.

Grants a large orbital similar to Teratoma that slowly orbits Isaac. The orbital repels enemies that touch it and deals 3.5 contact damage to enemies, once per second. After blocking 3 shots and/or dealing 3 ticks of contact damage, the orbital splits into two smaller ones that orbit opposite of each other.

Gives Isaac +5 bombs. Upon exploding, Isaac's bombs fire 4 Brimstone lasers in the cardinal directions. Each laser deals 8 damage per tick, and can hit up to 19 times. Multiple lasers cannot hit the same enemy simultaneously. The lasers cannot damage Isaac.
warunek: After breaking Hornfel's minecart, kill him before he can escape

cytat: Beat the juice out of them!
opis: Causes Isaac's active item to charge when Isaac deals damage to enemies, rather than when he clears rooms. Damage dealt by charmed/friendly enemies, active items, orbitals, and familiars (including blue flies and spiders) works as well. Charges are gained at a rate equal to the following formula: (40 + (20 × stage)). For example:

Gives Isaac a familiar that mimics Baby Plum's attack of shooting tears and propelling itself diagonally around the room. It deals 2 contact damage per tick to enemies (6 per second). It fires 15 tears per second behind itself while moving which deals 3 damage. Fruity Plum will prioritize moving towards nearby enemies.

On use, summons Baby Plum to fight for Isaac in the current room for ~10 seconds. Baby Plum will always twirl, jump, then spit blood and bounce around the room before waving goodbye and flying away. Baby Plum does 12 contact damage per tick (24 per second), 3.5 damage per shot, and 2 damage per tick with creep (20 per second).
warunek: Allow Baby Plum to escape instead of defeating her

opis: Spawns a star familiar that slowly travels toward the Boss Room, fading through doors as it travels towards it. Once it reaches the boss room, the familiar stands in the center of the boss room for the rest of the floor. The familiar emits an aura that, while Isaac stands inside it, gives him a 1.8× damage multiplier, a 2.5× fire rate multiplier, and homing tears.

Spawns an ice cube familiar. It cannot move on its own, but slides around when Isaac walks into it, uses a melee attack on it, or when Isaac or enemies shoot at it. The familiar damages and slows enemies it touches while moving. It can deal up to 11 damage when it collides with enemies, depending on how fast it's moving.

While held, enemies leave behind small, red ghosts on death; using the item causes the ghosts to explode, dealing damage to nearby enemies and destroying nearby enemy projectiles. The explosions will not destroy obstacles, move pickups, or damage Isaac.

Identifies all pills upon pickup. Spawns a pill. Grants one Black Heart on pickup. Converts all good pills into their bad counterparts. Grants +0.6 damage for each stat down pill and +1.2 damage for each stat down horse pill Isaac has taken, including those taken before acquiring False PHD. Using a non-stat down bad pill drops a Black Heart.

While the item is held, grants a spinning top orbital that blocks enemy shots and deals contact damage (3.5 per tick, or 35 per second). While the item is being used, gives Isaac +0.5 speed and increases the contact damage on Isaac's familiars, even if they do not normally deal any. Additionally, orbital familiars spin much faster and knock back both enemies and Isaac when they deal damage.

cytat: A king's fortune... but at what cost?
opis: Upon use, summons a sword that hangs directly above Isaac. As long as the sword hangs above Isaac, all item pedestals are doubled, spawning an additional free item next to them. Items spawned via other means, like Machines, s, and certain items and cards, are also doubled. The extra item will be pulled from the current room's item pool, even if the original item is from a different pool (ex.
warunek: Defeat The Lamb as Jacob and Esau

opis: Upon killing an enemy, 1 - 16 tears are released from them in random directions. The amount of tears released from an enemy is based on the enemy's health, and is not affected by Isaac's damage, nor the number of tears hit. These tears are red in coloration, regardless of Isaac's tears, but will inherit the coloration of applied status effects.

Whenever Isaac takes damage, he summons a micro-Isaac familiar, known as Minisaac, that chases nearby enemies and attacks on its own. Minisaacs deal 1.35 base damage that scales with +0.15 per floor: damage = 1.35 + 0.15 * (floor - 1) Minisaacs shoot 3 tears per second and their tear delay is not affected by Isaac's stats.
+2.5 Range. +0.22 tear size.

At the beginning of each floor, spawns 2 colored portals that each lead to either the Boss Room (red), Treasure Room (yellow), Secret Room (blue), or the Shop (green). On floors with no treasure rooms or no secret rooms, only 1 portal will spawn. The portals will disappear once the starting room is left, whether a portal was taken or not.

Upon killing enemies, spawns a stationary familiar in their place for the room, up to a maximum of 5. These familiars do not follow Isaac. The following familiars can spawn: Boiled Baby Brother Bobby Demon Baby Freezer Baby Ghost Baby Guardian Angel Harlequin Baby Lil Brimstone Little Steven Multidimensional Baby

Every 6 seconds, Isaac turns to stone and sprouts spikes for 1 second. While Isaac is in this state, he is immune to damage and deals 40 contact damage. Isaac flashes white three times before turning to stone on the fourth flash. The first three flashes are accompanied by a "ticking" sound, the final one with the spike extension sound of a mimic chest.

Grants one Red Heart Container and fully heals Isaac. Item pedestals cycle between the initial item and one of several food items. Upon picking up an affected food item, in addition to their normal effects, Isaac gains a temporary +3.6 Damage boost (which fades over 30 seconds), a permanent boost to 2 stats depending on the food item consumed, and a permanent -0.03 speed reduction.
Shows the contents of Chests, Fire Places, Grab Bags, and Shopkeepers before they're opened/destroyed.

Spawns Keeper as an extra character alongside Isaac, who is controlled exactly like Esau. Keeper will have his base stats, except speed, and use coins as health. He will not receive any of his starting items, such as Wooden Nickel or . When Keeper dies, he spawns 5 blue spiders and Strawman is removed from Isaac's inventory.
warunek: Defeat The Beast as Tainted Keeper

opis: Initiates the Ascent sequence. Any trinkets left in Treasure Rooms and Boss Rooms will turn into Cracked Keys.
Fully heals Isaac. Gives Isaac one Red Heart Container. +0.2 Speed. +0.5 Damage. +0.5 Tears. +2.5 Range. +0.16 Shot Speed. +1.0 Luck. +6.9% Devil Room / Angel Room chance. +6.9% Planetarium chance.
Upon completing a room, if a room clear reward would spawn, another different reward spawns next to it. If Isaac directly interacts with one, the other disappears.

Gives Isaac one of the following stat boosts per half heart healed with Red Heart pickups or when gaining an extra filled heart container: +0.02 speed. +0.05 tears. +0.10 flat damage. +0.25 range. +0.02 shot speed. +0.10 luck. Spawns a red heart when picked up.

Devil Deals and Black Market items can now be paid with Penny. Anything that generates Devil Deals (such as Satanic Bible or the special chests in Dark Room) will also create items that cost coins. Items in Shops now cost health. Consumables in shops have no cost, but are surrounded with spikes and can't be pushed out with an explosion.

Upon entering a Devil Room, a white inverted cross will appear above Isaac's head. If Isaac goes to the next floor with the icon still above his head, he will gain a soul heart and +1.0 damage. Taking anything generated by the room (items or consumables) will result in the icon going away, including opening Red Chests/Black Sacks.

Upon death, Isaac turns into a ghost that is linked to Isaac's dead body, similarly to The Soul, and can continue fighting with 1/2 of a heart. Isaac comes back to life 10 seconds after this effect occurs if he doesn't take damage while in ghost form. Isaac has flight in ghost form, but no spectral tears unlike other ghostly forms.
warunek: Defeat The Beast as The Forgotten

opis: Upon taking damage, activates the following effects: Unlocks all locked doors in the room (doors that need the room to be cleared are not affected). Opens the Secret Room and the Super Secret Room if adjacent to the room you are in. Reveals a random unmapped room on the floor. Destroys all tinted rocks and rocks with Crawl Spaces in the room.
If Isaac has one red heart or less at the beginning of a floor, an empty heart container is added.

The first time Isaac takes damage while in an uncleared room, time is stopped for 2 seconds and Isaac becomes a ghost and leaves his body behind, which drops to the floor. While in ghost form, Isaac has flight and spectral tears and the next hit he takes is negated. Isaac's tears and speed are greatly increased while time is stopped, although this is not shown on the HUD.

Isaac gains a short-delay charge attack that fires homing, piercing, and spectral fetus tears. The fetus tears do 75% of Isaac's damage, 3.75 times per second, until they run out of Range. The fetuses will automatically fire when Isaac is at full charge, meaning the charge cannot be held. Charge time is calculated based on Tear Delay.

Gives Isaac a familiar that creates a fluctuating black ring after a 1-second charge time that damages enemies that touch it, similarly to Maw Of The Void. The black ring lasts for 1 second and deals 3.5 damage per tick, up to 15 times per ring, for a total of 52.5 damage.

Continuously shooting for 2.35 seconds allows Isaac to fire a short-ranged brown Brimstone laser and corn shots behind himself by releasing the fire button. The laser deals Isaac's damage per tick (15× Isaac's damage per second), ticks up to 19 times, and lasts ~1.28 seconds (77 frames). Corn shots deal 6 damage, and a random amount are created each blast.

Summons a portal familiar that launches itself in the direction Isaac shoots to deal contact damage to enemies. The portal will consume all pickups and trinkets in its path, converting them into blue flies and causing the familiar to become larger and deal more contact damage. Lil Portal deals contact damage equal to (2x consumables destroyed count + 2), and ticks up to 10 times per second.

Gives Isaac a Nerve Ending familiar that burrows out of the ground to grab enemies for 4 seconds. Grabbed enemies take damage over time, are slowed, and are unable to move. Worm Friend deals 4 damage per tick (8 per second), for a total of 32 damage per grab. Worm Friend can target and restrain bosses in addition to normal enemies, even including endgame bosses such as Ultra Greed.

Adds a 10% chance to fire a booger tear that sticks to the first enemy that it comes in contact with and deals damage over time. Each booger deals damage equal to Isaac's tear damage every second. Increases all stats while Isaac's active item is fully charged: +0.25 speed. +0.75 range. +0.50 tear falling speed. +0.20 tears. +0.10 shot speed. +0.50 damage. +1 luck.

All pickups on the ground become their doubled variants as long as Isaac's held pickups are all the same number, e.g. 65 pennies, 65 bombs, 65 keys. It now only grants +4 fire rate.
